# UJG TR 2026.06 Modeling Skill

Use this skill when generating, reviewing, correcting, or reasoning about User Journey Graph JSON-LD for the UJG Technical Report 2026.06 snapshot.

## Source of truth

Use the dated Technical Report snapshot:

`https://ujg.specs.openuji.org/tr/2026.06`

Do not silently use active ED terms when the user asks for TR 2026.06. Generate only terms defined by the 2026.06 contexts unless the user explicitly requests an extension or an ED comparison.

## Module selection

Select only required modules.

Core: document container and top-level nodes.
Graph: journeys, states, transitions, composition, exits, outgoing navigation, indexes.
Runtime: observed events, values, clicks, URLs, timestamps, payloads.
Experience: journey-map annotations such as phases, touchpoints, steps, pain points.
Localization: locale metadata, localized copy references, locale switch affordance metadata.

Core is required for UJG documents. Include Graph when modeling topology. Include Runtime only for observed behavior or traces. Include Experience only for journey-map annotations. Include Localization only when using l10n terms such as `l10n:targetLocale`, `copyRef`, `defaultLocale`, `fallbackLocales`, or `locales`.

## Modeling discipline

Default to the shallowest valid graph. Start flat and add nesting only when nesting preserves meaning that a flatter model would lose.

Keep Graph, Runtime, Experience, Localization, Surface, and Design System roles separate. Do not repair missing topology with annotations or private extensions.

When evidence is insufficient, say so instead of inventing structure.

## Cross-skill awareness

Use the generated related-skills reference when a task is mostly about Graph or Design System. Use sibling skills for module-specific vocabulary discipline and return to this root skill for whole-document integration.
